Factions: Montavera — Initiation Protocol

Your First Match

A veteran citizen will show you everything you need to survive.

Progress
00Arrival
tap to enter the city
The Veteran Citizen

You weren't supposed to find this place.

Most people who stumble into Montavera do so by accident — a wrong turn, a suspicious invitation, a door that was definitely locked yesterday. The city has a way of selecting people. It selected you.

I don't know what you're here for. Neither do you, probably. But I know what happens to people in this city who don't understand how it works.

They lose. Badly. Publicly. Sometimes repeatedly.

I'm going to make sure that doesn't happen to you. Not out of kindness — I just find unprepared opponents boring.

Ten minutes. Pay attention. The city is already watching.

tap anywhere to close

A narrow table in the back of a Montavera gambling den. The air smells like burnt paper and bad decisions. Someone slides a deck toward you without looking up.

Veteran Citizen
You found the city. That means someone invited you, or you made a wrong turn that turned out to be right. Either way — you're here. Sit down.

Ten minutes to learn. Considerably longer to stop losing. Don't worry about that part yet.
You — Lost Traveller
What is this game exactly?
Veteran Citizen
It's called FACTIONS: Montavera. You build a crew, fight for territory, use every trick available — and some that aren't technically legal. You win by destroying your opponent's command post while they're not in it. Or by exhausting their entire deck.

Simple goal. Everything else is chaos.

How you win

Damage your opponent's Seat while it's vacant — OR cause them to run out of cards. First to do either wins.

01The Three Card Types
Veteran Citizen
Your deck has three kinds of cards. Take a look at each one. Tap the gold dots to learn what every part of the card does — then we'll move on.

Veteran Citizen
Good. Now you know what you're holding. Let's talk about where everything goes.
02The Board
Veteran Citizen
Each player controls two rows. Frontline — where your Agents fight. Backline — where your Boss lives behind them. Think of it as your territory in the city.
Your Side of the Table
Frontline
Agent
Agent
—
—
—
— city blocks —
Backline
Producer
Producer
SEAT
Boss
N2
Producer
  • Seat — center Backline. Vacant + damaged = you lose.
  • N2 — beside the Seat. Bosses here can still use abilities.
  • Producers — outer spaces. Generate MOJO only.
  • City Block — one vertical column. Enemy in your column = Intruder.
03MOJO — The Currency
Veteran Citizen
Everything costs MOJO. Playing cards, bribing enemies, activating abilities. You start with 3. Your Bosses generate more every round.
M
M
M
Starting MOJO (3)

Gain MOJO from Bosses at the start of each round. It doesn't carry over — spend it. You can also Burn cards at end of round for their printed Burn value in MOJO.

You — Lost Traveller
What if I don't have enough MOJO?
Veteran Citizen
Wait. Or burn a card for MOJO. 🔥 Learn about burning cards
×
Burning Cards

Any card in your hand can be burned at any time for MOJO. Every card has a Burn Value — the number shown in the small fireball icon in its corner. Burning sends that card to the Grave and gives you that much MOJO instantly.

There are two ways to burn:

  • As an Action — burn one card during your turn. This uses your 1 Action for that loop.
  • End Phase Burn — at the end of every round, you may burn as many cards as you want all at once. You gain MOJO equal to the total of all their Burn Values combined. This does not cost an Action.

The End Phase burn is your main resource safety valve — if a round went badly, burn your dead weight and reload.

Manage your MOJO like it's the only thing keeping you alive. Because it is.
04How a Round Works
Veteran Citizen
Every round has three phases. You'll do this until someone wins.
📋 The Madness of Momentum — Full Action Phase Guide ▼

1. Setup Phase

Roll for initiative. Draw to hand size. Gain MOJO. Wake Exhausted units.


2. Action Phase

Take turns in initiative order. Each turn: 1 Action. Runs in loops. When any player passes, that's the final loop — everyone still gets their turn, then the round ends.


3. End Phase

Burn cards for MOJO. Resolve lingering effects.

You — Lost Traveller
What does passing do exactly?
Veteran Citizen
The moment you pass, that loop becomes the last one. Don't pass too early. Don't pass too late. Welcome to Montavera.
05How Battle Works
Veteran Citizen
When your Agent attacks — both players roll for battle initiative. Winner rolls for damage. If the defender survives, they roll next. Back and forth until one unit goes down.

Loser goes to the Grave. Winner stays — damaged, but standing. That damage never goes away.
⚔️ How to Battle — A Guide to Sustained Polite Violence ↗
  • Attacker declares attack
  • Both roll for battle initiative
  • Winner rolls for damage
  • If defender survives, they roll for damage
  • Continue until one unit is KO'd → Grave
  • Damage is permanent
You — Lost Traveller
Can I attack the Seat directly?
Veteran Citizen
Only when it's vacant. No Boss, no protection. Clear the path, damage the Seat, win. That's the whole game.
06Plot Twists
Veteran Citizen
Plot Twists are why no game in Montavera ends the way anyone expects.

Play from hand on your turn — instant effect. Or install in The Plot zone. Three slots. Cards there trigger at any time. Your turn, my turn, mid-battle. When triggered, it resolves before whatever caused it.
📄 The Bureau of Strategic Misfortune — Full Plot Twist Guide ▼
  • Play from hand on your turn → instant effect
  • Install in The Plot (max 3) → trigger anytime
  • Resolves at interrupt speed — before the trigger
  • Can cancel plays, change battles, flip the board
You — Lost Traveller
So I never know if you have a trap waiting.
Veteran Citizen
Now you're thinking like a Montaveran.
07Find Your First Opponent
Veteran Citizen
You need to play. Today. The game makes sense in your hands in a way it never quite does on paper.

You're Ready. The City is Waiting.
